title: daphne pachyrachis
intro: Daphne pachyrachis, also known as the Himalayan daphne, is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and has been found to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Daphne pachyrachis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have been used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
